content.problemStatement: Identify and correct the logic to check if a number is even. The program should print `true` if number is even, else `false`.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output "true" if N is even, otherwise output "false". Each answer is printed on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 4, 6 and 8 are even → true; 7 and 9 are odd → false.
10 and 12 are even → true; 11 is odd → false.
1 is odd, so the answer is false.

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
false
true
false
true
false
true

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. For each test case read the integer N and check the remainder when dividing by 2. If N % 2 equals 0 the number is even and we output "true", otherwise we output "false". This uses constant time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in O(1) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used regardless of T.

code.Python3:
import sys

data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
out = []
idx = 1
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(data[idx])
    idx += 1
    out.append('true' if N % 2 == 0 else 'false')
print('\n'.join(out))
